Back to insights

Back to insights

Tutorial: Introduction to Formal Verification with Lean (Part 1)

Formal verification is a tool for checking mathematical statements in code. This tutorial introduces Lean to cryptographic engineers and develops a complete example.

The Lean programming language & proof assistant

Lean is a functional programming language and theorem prover. It can express axioms, lemmas, theorems, and machine-checked proofs.

Tutorial

The tutorial has four parts after a small hello-world example. Every example is intended to be entered and checked by the reader.

Hello world in Lean

Write the following expressions and verify their outcomes in the information view.

#eval "Hello World!"
#eval String.append "Hello " "World!"
#eval String.append "Hello " (if 1 > 2 then "Lean!" else "World!")

Tutorial Part 1: Define a BitString and XOR

The one-time pad is defined over keys, messages, and ciphertexts that are bit strings of the same length.

import Mathlib.Data.ZMod.Basic
def BitString (L: ℕ): Type := Vector (ZMod 2) L

Epilogue: About Formal Verification in blockchain

Formal verification can increase confidence in software, although the relationship between a model and compiled implementation must still be considered carefully.
